 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kkfn","title":"FEATURE: Documentation & Learning Hub Updates","description":"## Parent Epic
agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa (Beginner UX Overhaul v1.0)

## Summary

Update all documentation (Learning Hub lessons, README, in-code comments) to reflect
the new password-first SSH flow and enhanced onboarding experience.

## Background & Motivation

After implementing the password-first flow and other UX improvements, all existing
documentation will be outdated. Users who read lessons or README before/during setup
would get conflicting information.

This feature ensures documentation consistency across all touchpoints.

## Scope

### 1. Learning Hub Lessons

Update the 9 lessons in /content/lessons/:

| Lesson | Changes Needed |
|--------|---------------|
| 00_welcome.md | Update overview of setup process |
| 01_linux_basics.md | No changes needed |
| 02_ssh_basics.md | Update SSH key flow explanation |
| 03_tmux_basics.md | No changes needed |
| 04_agents_login.md | Update auth instructions |
| 05_ntm_core.md | No changes needed |
| 06_ntm_command_palette.md | No changes needed |
| 07_flywheel_loop.md | No changes needed |
| 08_keeping_updated.md | No changes needed |

Key updates:
- Lesson 2 (SSH Basics): Explain password-first approach
- Lesson 4 (Agent Commands): Update auth expectations

### 2. README.md Updates

Current README mentions SSH setup - update to reflect:
- Password-first approach
- Simplified wizard flow
- "Your First 5 Minutes" after install

### 3. "Practice Now" Sections

Add to each lesson a practical exercise that users can do on their VPS:

\`\`\`markdown
## Practice This Now

SSH into your VPS and try these commands:
1. \`ls -la ~/\` - List your home directory
2. \`pwd\` - Show current directory
3. \`cd /tmp && pwd\` - Navigate to tmp

When you've tried these, come back and click "Mark Complete".
\`\`\`

This bridges the gap between web-based learning and terminal practice.

### 4. Quick Reference Updates

Update the reference pages:
- /learn/commands - Ensure SSH commands reflect new flow
- /learn/glossary - Add any new terms

### 5. Inline Documentation

Update code comments in:
- install.sh - Explain SSH key prompt
- scripts/lib/user.sh - Document key migration flow
- Wizard page components - Update JSDoc comments

## Content Guidelines

### Tone for Beginners
- Assume zero prior knowledge
- Explain "why" not just "what"
- Use analogies (SSH key = house key + lock)
- Acknowledge when something is scary/confusing

### Command Examples
- Always show expected output
- Include copy buttons
- Add troubleshooting for common errors

### Visual Consistency
- Use same component library (CommandCard, AlertCard)
- Consistent color coding
- Mobile-friendly layouts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All lessons accurate for new flow
- [ ] README reflects password-first approach
- [ ] Practice sections in relevant lessons
- [ ] Quick reference pages updated
- [ ] No conflicting information across docs
- [ ] All code comments updated

## Dependencies

- ALL other features in this epic
- This should be done LAST to ensure accuracy

## Testing Plan

1. Full read-through of all lessons
2. Follow documentation to set up fresh VPS
3. Check for any conflicting instructions
4. Get feedback from actual beginner

## Stretch Goals

- Video walkthroughs for each major step
- Interactive tutorials embedded in wizard
- Discord community link for support","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:18:22.045361-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:53:13.646623-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:53:13.646623-05:00","close_reason":"Documentation & Learning Hub updates completed - SSH lessons, agents lesson, and README updated for password-first flow. Practice sections added. All quality gates pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kkfn","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t66c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:18:30.197548-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kkfn","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-trkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:18:30.432668-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kkfn","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-rn2q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:18:30.596682-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kkfn","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b1yk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:18:30.771819-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kmmi","title":"fix(session): make sanitize_session_export atomic","description":"scripts/lib/session.sh sanitize_session_export claimed atomic replace but created the temp file in /var/folders/vt/n2xyn_s51b97_j3yh2qbqcnc0000gn/T/, so mv could become cross-device (non-atomic) and risk partial writes/corruption. Create the temp file in the same directory as the target file so rename is truly atomic.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-25T07:39:34.441857-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T07:39:38.216909-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T07:39:38.216909-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: create temp file alongside target for atomic rename; shellcheck clean.","labels":["reliability","security"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kozt","title":"fix(installer): pin bootstrap to resolved commit + correct SUDO default","description":"Fixes two reliability footguns:\n\n1) curl|bash mode could bootstrap the repo tarball from a moving ref (e.g. main) and later resolve a different commit SHA for resume/versioning, risking mixed-ref resumes after Ubuntu auto-upgrade. Resolve the ref to a specific commit SHA early and use it for ACFS_REF/ACFS_RAW + bootstrap.\n\n2) scripts/lib/user.sh and scripts/lib/zsh.sh used : "sudo" which forces SUDO="sudo" even when running as root. Set SUDO="" when EUID==0 to match library contract.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-29T02:41:29.192523-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T02:42:09.429413-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T02:42:09.429413-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kt6v","title":"Deep audit: random-walk code tracing (phase 2)","description":"Randomly explore and trace flows across installer libs, manifest generator, onboarding, and web; find obvious bugs/security issues; fix and push.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T15:04:10.198398-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T15:26:19.559759-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T15:26:19.559759-05:00","close_reason":"Completed phase-2 random-walk audit; no additional high-confidence bugs found."}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwa","title":"Fail-fast remote curl|bash invocations in install.sh","description":"Several remote installer invocations in install.sh run via subshells without pipefail (e.g., oh-my-zsh uses sh -c with curl output, and multiple run_as_target bash -c "curl ... | sh"). If curl fails, the subshell can still return success and the installer continues. Fix by running these under bash with set -euo pipefail and passing args safely, so download/exec failures abort correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T15:17:16.089137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T15:20:45.480511-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T15:20:45.480511-05:00","close_reason":"Wrap remote installer subshells with set -euo pipefail so curl failures abort; also make stack remote installer helper pass args safely and avoid masking download errors.","labels":["installer","reliability"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kwk","title":"Vendor NTM command_palette.md for onboarding","description":"# Task: Vendor NTM Command Palette

## Description
Copy and maintain a local version of NTM's command_palette.md for use in onboarding.

## Background
The onboard TUI needs to show NTM's prompt palette to users. Rather than fetch it live (requires internet), we vendor a copy.

## Implementation

### Vendor Location
`acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Sync Script
`scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh`

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Sync NTM command palette from upstream

SOURCE="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/main/command_palette.md"
DEST="acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/command_palette.md"

curl -fsSL "$SOURCE" > "$DEST"
echo "Synced command_palette.md"
```

### Install Location
Deployed to: `~/.acfs/docs/ntm/command_palette.md`

### Onboard Integration
In Mission 6, show the palette using:
- bat (pretty) if available
- less as fallback

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sync script works
- [ ] Palette included in installer
- [ ] Displayed in onboard Mission 6
- [ ] Readable over SSH","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:28:15.931606-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:22:39.515282-05:00","close_reason":"Vendored NTM command_palette.md to acfs/onboard/docs/ntm/. Added sync script scripts/sync/sync_ntm_palette.sh with --check mode. Commit 2a09af8.","labels":["onboard","task"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kyhv","title":"TASK: Add lesson completion celebration screen","description":"# TASK: Add lesson completion celebration screen

## Context
When user completes a lesson, show a brief celebration before moving to next.

## Proposed Screen
```
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│  🎉 Lesson Complete: tmux Basics                            │
│                                                             │
│  You learned:                                               │
│  • Creating sessions with ntm new                           │
│  • Attaching/detaching with Ctrl+B, D                       │
│  • Listing sessions with ntm list                           │
│                                                             │
│  Progress: ████████████░░░░░░░░ 5/9 (56%)                  │
│                                                             │
│  [Enter] Continue to next lesson                            │
│  [m] Return to menu                                         │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Implementation

### Lesson Summary Data
Add to each lesson markdown a summary section:
```markdown
<!-- SUMMARY
- Creating sessions with ntm new
- Attaching/detaching with Ctrl+B, D
- Listing sessions with ntm list
-->
```

Or define in onboard.sh as associative array.

### Celebration Function
```bash
onboard_show_completion() {
  local lesson_num=$1
  local lesson_title=$(get_lesson_title $lesson_num)
  local summary=$(get_lesson_summary $lesson_num)
  # Render celebration screen
  # Wait for keypress
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Celebration shown after lesson completion
- [ ] Shows lesson title and summary points
- [ ] Shows updated progress bar
- [ ] Enter continues, m returns to menu
- [ ] Works with gum and plain fallback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:59:49.050712-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:23:43.155042-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:23:43.155042-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented celebration screen with lesson summaries, progress bar, gum UI with fallback, and next/menu navigation","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kyhv","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-gjnn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T15:00:11.532879-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
